title: Share who are worried about vs. share of deaths from terrorism
subtitle: Respondents who said they worried "very much" or "a great deal". Confirmed deaths, including all victims and attackers who died because of a [terrorist attack](#dod:terrorism).
caption:
Share who are worried about vs. share of deaths from terrorism

Respondents who said they worried "very much" or "a great deal". Confirmed deaths, including all victims and attackers who died because of a terrorist attack.

This scatter plot maps countries based on the share of people worried about terrorism on the vertical axis against the share of deaths from terrorism on a logarithmic horizontal axis. The vertical scale ranges from 30% to over 90%, while the horizontal axis spans from 0.0001% to 1%. Countries are color-coded by region, with Asia and Oceania in teal, Africa in purple, Europe in blue, South America in brown, and North America in orange. Circle size corresponds to the number of attacks, with a legend indicating scales for 200 and 500 attacks. Iraq and Nigeria appear in the upper right quadrant, with worry shares between 80% and 90% and death shares near or above 0.1%. Myanmar shows a worry share exceeding 90% but a death share below 0.01%. In contrast, countries like the Netherlands, Canada, and New Zealand cluster in the lower left, with worry shares between 25% and 35% and death shares below 0.001%. A large group of nations, including the United Kingdom, Germany, and Brazil, occupy the middle-left area, displaying worry levels between 40% and 60% alongside very low death shares around 0.001%. India and Kuwait sit distinctly apart; India has a worry share near 60% with a death share around 0.002%, while Kuwait shows a worry share near 60% with a death share approaching 0.1%. The distribution reveals no linear correlation, as high worry levels exist across the entire spectrum of death shares.
